title: TranSQL + : Serving Large Language Models with SQL on Low-Resource Hardware
importance_reason: 关注延迟，涉及向量化执行，涉及连接处理
abstract: Deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) on resource-constrained devices remains challenging due to limited memory, lack of GPUs, and the complexity of existing runtimes. In this paper, we introduce TranSQL + , a template-based code generator that translates LLM computation graphs into pure SQL queries for execution in relational databases. Without relying on external libraries, TranSQL + , leverages mature database features-such as vectorized execution and out-of-core processing-for efficient inference. We further propose a row-to-column (ROW2COL) optimization that improves join efficiency in matrix operations. Evaluated on Llama3-8B and DeepSeekMoE models, TranSQL + achieves up to 20× lower prefill latency and 4× higher decoding speed compared to DeepSpeed Inference and Llama.cpp in low-memory and CPU-only configurations. Our results highlight relational databases as a practical environment for LLMs on low-resource hardware.
